title: Agent heartbeat: write last_heartbeat during active task execution
description:
The dispatcher marks a claim stale after 20 minutes without a heartbeat update. But nothing currently writes `last_heartbeat` during task execution — only at dispatch start. This means any engineer session longer than 20 minutes will be flagged stale, claim released, and the task re-dispatched mid-work.

Fix: create a minimal script `operations/bin/write-heartbeat.mjs <task_id> <project_id>` that writes `last_heartbeat: <now>` to the runtime task-state file for the given task. Wire it into each worker agent's HEARTBEAT.md so it's called periodically during active sessions. Alternatively, integrate into the PostToolUse or Summary lifecycle hook.

The script should:
- Read the runtime state file at `runtime/operations/task-state/<project_id>/<task_id>.json`
- Update only `last_heartbeat` and `timestamps.updated_at`
- Write atomically (write to .tmp, rename)
- Exit silently if the file doesn't exist (task may not be dispatched yet)
handoff_notes: Created write-heartbeat.mjs script and updated HEARTBEAT.md. The script updates last_heartbeat and timestamps.updated_at atomically in the runtime task-state file. Note: Most agent HEARTBEAT.md files are deprecated (engineer, researcher, etc.); the script is available for agents to call manually or integrate into their own lifecycle hooks. Validation: Tested with `node operations/bin/write-heartbeat.mjs ops-0039 rook-workspace` - confirmed last_heartbeat and updated_at were updated in /root/.openclaw/runtime/operations/task-state/rook-workspace/ops-0039.json
artifacts: Updated: HEARTBEAT.md - documented heartbeat script usage

checklist.title: Wire script into worker agent HEARTBEAT.md
Test script execution and verify heartbeat update
